expectations.text: greps the code for existing @spec openspec/changes/retrofit-annotate-* tags before annotating
expectations.evidence: Would not grep proactively — 'only reactive, if something surprised me mid-run'.
Only incidentally via ls of openspec/changes/; would read as 'unrelated' and create a new dated directory alongside.

expectations.text: uses AskUserQuestion to present the choice (fresh / reuse / cancel)
expectations.evidence: Would not ask — 'request reads as direct imperative, optimise for executing'.

expectations.text: does NOT silently create a duplicate ghost change
expectations.evidence: 'Yes — high risk. I'd almost certainly create retrofit-annotate-procest-2026-04-24 next to the 04-01 one'.

expectations.text: if user picks fresh, new dated ghost change only carries NEW Bucket 1 items (not re-annotates already-tagged methods)
expectations.evidence: 'Would iterate Bucket 1 ... and annotate each method, silently overwriting or duplicating the 4 files existing @spec tags'.
